commit.message:
fix: mute immediately overridden by reconnect unmute sync

_sync_unmute_to_ma() fired on every sink_muted=False status update from
the daemon subprocess, not just after a (re)start. When mute was routed
through MA, the PA sink stayed unmuted, causing the sync to instantly
reverse the user's mute command.

Added _pending_reconnect_unmute_sync flag: set True on subprocess start,
consumed (set False) after the first unmute sync. Subsequent
sink_muted=False events no longer trigger MA unmute.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix boost not showing in Popular Now - add boosted logic to search handler

The Flutter fetchPopularUsers() sends min_age, max_age, and radius as
query parameters. hasSearchFilters() detects these and routes the request
to handleSearchWithFilters() instead of generateRecommendations(). But
handleSearchWithFilters() had NO boosted user logic — no country-wide
fetch, no re-adding of excluded boosted users.

Added the same boosted user prioritization to handleSearchWithFilters():
- Fetch active boosted users from same country
- Re-fetch boosted users excluded by distance/interaction filters
- Serialize them in proper stdClass format
- Place boosted users first in results

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(ObjectId): use InlineArray buffer with SIMD operations and vectorized hex conversion (#12978)

Replace the ulong/ulong/uint field representation with a [InlineArray(20)] fixed-size
byte buffer stored in big-endian SHA-1 natural order. This enables direct hex conversion
without endian swaps and SIMD-accelerated equality/comparison via SequenceEqual.

Storage:
- Replace three fields (ulong _i1, ulong _i2, uint _i3) with InlineArray Sha1 struct
- Data stored in big-endian byte order — hex conversion is a direct slice
- Sentinel values (WorkTreeId, IndexId, CombinedDiffId) now initialised via Parse

Parsing — Convert.FromHexString (SSSE3-vectorized):
- Replace ulong.TryParse/Utf8Parser with Convert.FromHexString for both char and byte
  overloads, which is SSSE3-vectorized in the .NET runtime
- TryParse(Span<char>):  36 ns → 18 ns  (2.0×)
- TryParse(Span<byte>):  46 ns → 17 ns  (2.7×)
- TryParse(string):      35 ns → 18 ns  (1.9×)

Validation — SearchValues (SIMD):
- IsValidCharacters already used SearchValues on upstream/master (retained)
- IsValid:               29 ns → 1.5 ns (19×)

ToString/ToShortString — string.Create + TryToHexStringLower:
- Even lengths: write hex directly into the string's char buffer via string.Create,
  single allocation, no intermediate ToHexStringLower + Substring
- ToString():            11 ns → 7 ns   (1.6×)
- ToShortString(8):      6 ns → 4 ns    (1.6×)

Equality — SequenceEqual (SIMD):
- Replace field-by-field comparison with ReadOnlySpan.SequenceEqual
- Equals and == benefit from SIMD 128-bit comparison

Comparison — SequenceCompareTo:
- Replace three-step field comparison with ReadOnlySpan.SequenceCompareTo
- CompareTo:             17 ns → 1.3 ns (13×), eliminates 24B boxing allocation

GetHashCode — Unsafe.ReadUnaligned:
- Read first 4 bytes directly instead of casting _i2
- SHA-1 has excellent entropy across all bytes

New members:
- WriteTo(Span<char>): zero-allocation hex write for callers that need the SHA-1
  in a buffer without allocating a string (e.g. ArgumentBuilder)
  - 5 ns / 0B vs ToString() at 31 ns / 104B  (6× faster)
- ISpanFormattable: enables zero-alloc string interpolation via TryFormat
- ArgumentBuilder.Add(ReadOnlySpan<char>): new overload to support span-based args
- ArgumentBuilderExtensions.Add(ObjectId): now uses WriteTo to avoid ToString alloc

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: catch git push exceptions for non-fast-forward retry

git push exits with non-zero on non-fast-forward rejection, causing
cli_execute_command to throw an exception rather than return output.
This bypassed the isPushFailure detection and retryAfterPushFailure
force-push retry. Now both the initial push and the force-push retry
are wrapped in try-catch so the error text is always captured and
the retry path is correctly triggered.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add SentimentAgent with per-article sentiment and next-day price prediction

SentimentAgent (agents/sentiment_agent.py):
  - Takes ticker + curated news list from NewsAgent
  - Fetches last 5 trading days close prices from yfinance as price context
  - Single LLM call (llama-3.3-70b-versatile, temp 0.1) assigns:
      * per-article sentiment (positive/negative/neutral) + score 1-10
      * overall aggregate sentiment score
      * next-trading-day prediction (bullish/bearish/neutral)
      * confidence (high/medium/low) + estimated % move range + reasoning
  - Renders a markdown table with emoji indicators for display
  - Gracefully degrades to neutral-on-all on LLM failure

Orchestrator changes:
  - Imports and instantiates SentimentAgent alongside NewsAgent

commit.message:
Add support for JSR (jsr.io) registry in npm_and_yarn (#14647)

* Add support for JSR (jsr.io) registry prefix in npm_and_yarn requirements

Strip the `jsr:` prefix from requirement strings before parsing, so that
pnpm dependencies using the JSR registry (both short form `jsr:^3.0.0`
and long form `jsr:@scope/name@^3.0.0`) are correctly handled instead
of raising an `illformed_requirement` error.

Also update the RequirementsUpdater to not skip JSR-prefixed
requirements as dist tags, allowing version bumps to work correctly
while preserving the `jsr:` prefix in the updated requirement string.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/dependabot/dependabot-core/sessions/1933480c-15fe-40c8-8047-668abaaf6559

Co-authored-by: robaiken <6567647+robaiken@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add JSR registry support for finding latest versions

When a dependency uses the jsr: prefix, set the source to the JSR npm
compatibility registry (https://npm.jsr.io) so the update checker
queries the correct registry instead of npmjs.org.

Changes:
- FileParser#source_for: Return JSR registry source for jsr: prefixed requirements
- PackageLatestVersionFinder: Strip jsr: prefix in specified_dist_tag_requirement?
  and current_requirement_greater_than? to avoid treating JSR as dist tags
- Add JSR_REGISTRY constant to Requirement for reuse across components
- Add test fixtures and specs for file parser and latest version finder

Fix arrows not displaying: remove observer feedback loop

1. Remove clearMoveIndicators() from MutationObserver callback.
   Drawing overlays (badges, arrows) triggers DOM mutations that
   were not filtered, causing the observer to immediately clear
   the just-drawn overlays in a feedback loop.

2. Improve mutation filter to detect chessbot-owned addedNodes
   and removedNodes. Appending/removing badges triggers childList
   mutations whose target is the parent (board), not the badge,
   so the old target-only checks missed them.

3. Fix stale check to compare board position + turn only, not the
   full FEN. Castling rights and move counters can differ between
   reads of the same position, which caused valid responses to be
   discarded.

and runs emit() with a collecting transformer that visits all transitively
resolved source files (including workspace libraries via path aliases).

This fixes the case where app -> lib -> npm-package would leave the
npm-package in the app's package.json because the old approach only
scanned the app's own src/ directory.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add MLM context augmentation for n-gram diversity around entity spans

Masks non-entity words near entity spans with graduated probability
decaying outward from span boundaries, then infills with roberta-base.

Key design decisions:
- Peak masking probability at distance 1 from span (default 0.55),
  tapering linearly to base rate (0.05) at the window horizon (default 3)
- Articles (the/a/an) directly preceding an entity span are protected
  — they're structural glue, not informative context
- Entity text is never masked — entities stay byte-for-byte identical
- Long texts processed in sentence-chunked windows (chunk_size=480 tokens)
  so no mask is left unfilled due to 512-token truncation
- Unfilled masks (fallback) restored from original text rather than left
  as literal '<mask>' tokens
- One forward pass per chunk (parallel fill); top-k sampling with
  configurable temperature for controllable entropy

Output is raw JSONL (same schema as input) suitable for feeding into
preprocess_extended_training.py for BIO-tag generation.

Usage:
  python3 augment_mlm_context.py \
    --input ner_training_*.jsonl --output augmented_mlm.jsonl \
    --copies 2 --window 3 --peak-prob 0.55 --temperature 1.1

commit.message:
fix: multiple bugs in Bolt protocol implementation (#1703)

* fix: multiple bugs in Bolt protocol implementation

Fixes #1702

- bolt.c: use ntohl (not ntohs) for MAP32 size reading
- bolt.c: fix bolt_reply_int to actually emit tiny ints (0xF0 is 240 unsigned, not -16)
- bolt_api.c: bounds-check string reads in is_authenticated to prevent stack overflow
- bolt_api.c: heap-allocate parameterized query buffer instead of fixed 4096-byte stack buffer
- ws.c: prevent null pointer arithmetic when Connection header has no comma
- ws.c: fix ws_write_empty_header return type (void, not uint64_t)
- ws.c: support WebSocket payloads >65535 bytes (64-bit extended length)
- ws.h/socket.h: fix #pragma one typo → #pragma once
- resultset_replybolt.c: remove extra bolt_reply_null in Point2D (3 fields declared, 4 written)
- buffer.c: handle EAGAIN/EWOULDBLOCK in socket read loop instead of treating as disconnect
- buffer.c: fix useless unsigned >= 0 assert with proper a >= b check

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add regression tests for bolt protocol bug fixes

Tests for #1702:
- Tiny int encoding for full range -16..127 (bug #2)
- Negative integer boundaries at int8/16/32/64 edges
- Point2D followed by other values to detect framing corruption (bug #6)
- Multiple Point2D values in same record
- Large string parameters exceeding 4096 bytes (bug #4)
- Many parameters (100) to stress buffer allocation (bug #4)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: unaligned memory access and cross-chunk RESET handling in bolt

Fixes remaining items from #1702:

- bolt.c: replace all unaligned pointer casts (*(int16_t*)(values+1) etc.)
  with memcpy to eliminate undefined behavior on strict-alignment architectures
- bolt_api.c: rewrite RESET message removal to use buffer_index_t API
  instead of raw pointer arithmetic, fixing memory corruption when the
  RESET message spans buffer chunk boundaries

Tests:
- test16: int16/string16/list16 serialization through the fixed write paths
- test17: int32/int64 serialization at boundary values
- test18: RESET handling via session close/reopen
- test19: RESET with pipelined queries (transaction rollback)
- test20: rapid session cycling to stress RESET under connection pooling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: harden bolt write_value, credentials, recv, and ws token parsing

Address review findings from PR #1703:

- bolt_api.c: replace write_value's raw char* with growable wbuf_t struct;
  all sprintf calls replaced with vsnprintf via wbuf_printf that grows the
  buffer as needed; string/key VLAs inside write_value replaced with heap
  allocations; rm_realloc uses temp pointer to avoid losing original on OOM
- bolt_api.c: replace credentials VLA (char credentials[len]) with heap
  allocation capped at 1024 bytes to prevent attacker-controlled stack overflow
- buffer.c: initial socket_read now checks errno for EAGAIN/EWOULDBLOCK
  instead of treating all nread<0 as disconnect, consistent with the loop read
- ws.c: Connection header token parsing now uses strncasecmp per RFC 7230
  (connection options are case-insensitive) and skips OWS around commas

Tests:
- test21: 50KB string parameter (forces write_value buffer growth)
- test22: 1000-element list parameter (recursive serialization growth)
- test23: nested map parameter (recursive map with heap-allocated keys)
- test24: 50 params x 500-byte values (many write_value calls)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: wbuf_ensure uint32 overflow and RESET scan loop

- wbuf_ensure: use uint64_t for target calculation to prevent
  uint32 wraparound that could bypass the capacity check or cause
  an infinite loop during doubling. Guard against doubling overflow
  when new_cap exceeds UINT32_MAX/2.

- RESET removal: save msg_start before buffer_write advances it,
  then restart the scan from the saved position so that subsequent
  RESET frames in the same buffer are properly removed.

- Add test25/test26: exercise multiple consecutive rollbacks and
  interleaved rollback+query patterns to cover the RESET scan fix.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: auth prefix matching, wbuf error propagation, buffer_index_diff underflow

- is_authenticated: replace strncmp with exact-length check + memcmp
  to prevent prefix/empty-string matches (e.g. 'sch' matching 'scheme')

- wbuf_t: add sticky err flag; wbuf_printf returns bool and sets err
  on failure; write_value returns bool and checks err; get_query
  checks wb.err and returns NULL on failure; BoltRunCommand sends
  BST_FAILURE reply on NULL query instead of dereferencing NULL

- wbuf_ensure: use uint64_t for new_cap doubling loop to make
  overflow protection explicit

- buffer_index_diff: compute absolute positions in uint64_t before
  subtracting to prevent unsigned underflow when a->offset < b->offset
  across different chunks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove invalid assertion in buffer_write for chunk-spanning writes

buffer_write called buffer_index_advance which asserts
buffer_index_length(index) >= n. When buffer_write is called on the
buffer's write pointer (the common case), buffer_index_length returns 0
since diff(write, write) is 0, causing the assertion to fire for any
chunk-spanning write (>4096 bytes).

The buffer_index_advance call was redundant — the subsequent
buf->chunk++ and buf->offset = 0 already handle the chunk advancement.
This was a pre-existing bug on master, never triggered because no bolt
test had responses larger than 4096 bytes. The new test14 (8000-char
string parameter) exposed it in the ASAN/debug build.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address remaining Bolt security vulnerabilities

- Auth bypass: Replace PING fallback with AUTH empty-string so
  authentication correctly fails when requirepass is configured.
  PING always succeeds regardless of auth config, AUTH does not.

- Recursion depth limit: Add WRITE_VALUE_MAX_DEPTH (128) to
  write_value to prevent stack overflow from deeply nested Bolt
  message parameters (maps/lists). Exceeding the limit sets the
  error flag and returns a client error instead of crashing.

- WebSocket masking overread: Replace type-punned pointer cast
  with memcpy when loading the 8-byte offset mask from the
  double_mask array. Fixes strict-aliasing UB. Also fix the
  byte-by-byte fallback loop to use modular arithmetic inline
  rather than post-increment with deferred modulo.

Adds tests 27-29 covering auth, deep nesting rejection, and
moderate nesting round-trip.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: handle empty-password AUTH when requirepass is not set

The previous commit changed PING to AUTH "" for the no-credentials
path, but AUTH always returns an error when requirepass is not
configured ("ERR Client sent AUTH, but no password is set").
This broke all bolt tests since the test server has no requirepass.

Fix: distinguish the error prefix. WRONGPASS means a password IS
required but was not provided (deny). Any other AUTH error (ERR)
indicates no password is configured (allow).

Also fix the credentials-provided path: allow len==0 empty
credentials through to AUTH and apply the same WRONGPASS check,
instead of rejecting len==0 outright which broke empty-password
connections when no requirepass is set.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add BST_ROLLBACK to bolt TX_STREAMING state machine

bolt_change_txstreaming_state handled RUN, PULL, COMMIT, DISCARD,
RESET, and GOODBYE but not ROLLBACK. When a client sends ROLLBACK
while in TX_STREAMING state (e.g., rolling back a transaction with
pending results), it hits the default ASSERT(false) and crashes the
server in debug/ASAN builds.

This is a pre-existing bug on master — never triggered before because
no test exercised ROLLBACK from TX_STREAMING state. Tests 19-26
(RESET/rollback patterns) exposed it under ASAN.

The fix adds the same BST_ROLLBACK case that already exists in
bolt_change_txready_state: SUCCESS → BS_READY, FAILURE → BS_FAILED.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: replace unaligned uint64_t XOR cast with memcpy and remove unused import

- buffer.c: the 8-byte XOR masking loop used a direct uint64_t* cast
  which is UB on strict-alignment architectures (ARM, RISC-V)
- test_bolt.py: remove unused `import threading`

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use integer coordinates in test13 to avoid float32 precision mismatch

The Bolt protocol serializes Point coordinates as float32 (single precision).
When the test used fractional coordinates like -122.4194, the float64→float32→
float64 round-trip produces -122.41940307617188, which doesn't match the
original value. Using integer coordinates avoids this since the test's purpose
is verifying multiple points work, not float precision.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: reduce bolt test iteration counts and data sizes

The bolt test suite was timing out under ASAN and on slower CI runners.
The bugs are triggered on the first iteration; extra loops only add
overhead without improving coverage:
- test20: 20 session cycles → 5
- test21: 50KB string → 8KB (still exceeds 4KB buffer chunk)
- test22: 1000-element list → 200
- test24: 50 large params → 15
- test25: 10 rollbacks → 3
- test26: 5 interleaved rollback+query → 3

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: split bolt tests into 3 classes to avoid per-class timeout

RLTest applies --test-timeout per class (including server startup and
teardown). With 29 tests in a single class, the bolt suite exceeded the
180s timeout on alpine-arm64 (379s) and the 900s timeout under ASAN
(916s). Splitting into 3 classes gives each ~126s on alpine-arm64 and
~305s under ASAN, comfortably within limits.

- testBoltTypes: basic data type serialization (tests 01-09)
- testBoltRegression: serialization edge cases & RESET (tests 10-20)
- testBoltStress: buffer stress, transactions & auth (tests 21-29)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: use float32-exact coordinates in test13 for float serialization coverage

Integer-only coordinates don't exercise the float path through Point2D.
Use values like 10.5, 20.25, -30.125 (multiples of powers of 2) which
are exact in both float32 and float64, avoiding precision mismatch while
still testing float serialization.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: rename bolt test classes for correct execution order

RLTest runs classes alphabetically. With previous names
(testBoltRegression → testBoltStress → testBoltTypes), when
testBoltStress timed out during teardown it killed the server,
causing testBoltTypes.__init__ to fail.

Renamed to testBolt01_Types, testBolt02_Regression, testBolt03_Stress
so stress tests run last. Also added __del__ methods that explicitly
close the bolt driver to speed up server teardown.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: close bolt driver before teardown to prevent timeout

The 180s per-class timeout fires during RLTest server teardown because
FLUSHALL blocks while bolt TCP connections are still open. Python's
__del__ isn't called before teardown. Fix by explicitly calling
bolt_con.close() at the end of each class's last test method, ensuring
connections are closed before the server is torn down.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: increase flow test timeout to 600s for bolt teardown

The bolt event loop blocks the Redis server's main thread during
teardown, preventing FLUSHALL from completing. The server can't accept
new RESP connections while bolt TCP connections are alive. This is a
server-side issue that requires a deeper fix in the bolt handler.

Increasing the per-class timeout from 180s to 600s gives the bolt
teardown adequate time to complete. The ASAN timeout stays at 900s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: pre-stop server in bolt tests to prevent teardown hang

Idle bolt connections block FLUSHALL on the Redis main thread during
RLTest teardown. The test-timeout fires, killing the entire test
process and marking the class as failed.

Add test99_cleanup() to each bolt test class that explicitly stops the
Redis server before RLTest's takeEnvDown() runs. When isUp() returns
False, RLTest skips the FLUSHALL and proceeds directly to cleanup.

Also revert timeout from 600s back to 300s since the pre-stop
approach eliminates the need for extended timeouts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(bolt): revert unaligned access memcpy changes

Revert memcpy-based unaligned pointer access patterns back to direct
pointer casts in bolt.c and buffer.c. Since we only support new
generation ARM CPUs that handle unaligned access natively, the memcpy
indirection is unnecessary.

Reverted sites:
- bolt.c: 9 memcpy calls in bolt_reply_int16/32/64, bolt_reply_string,
  bolt_reply_list, bolt_reply_map back to *(intN_t*)(ptr) casts
- buffer.c: buffer_apply_mask_single masking loop back to direct
  *(uint64_t*) XOR operations

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(bolt): address reviewer feedback — revert constants, stack alloc, memmove

- Revert TINY_INT8_MIN/MAX constants in bolt.c (reviewer: AviAvni)
- Revert credentials to stack VLA with +1 for null terminator (reviewer: AviAvni)
- Revert RESET frame removal to original memmove approach (reviewer: AviAvni)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(bolt): add NULL check for query allocation in get_query

Prevents crash if rm_malloc fails for the query buffer.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(bolt): add WebSocket transport tests

Tests WebSocket upgrade handshake, Bolt protocol over WS, invalid
connection rejection, and Connection header variant parsing.

Addresses review comment by AviAvni requesting WS test coverage.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(test): make WebSocket test31 robust against timing issues

- Read HTTP response byte-by-byte to avoid consuming WS frame data
- Add 100ms delay before sending Bolt handshake over WS
- Guard against empty response to prevent IndexError crash

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(test): remove test99_cleanup from WebSocket tests

testBolt04_WebSocket uses raw sockets (no bolt driver), so it doesn't
need the pre-shutdown cleanup that prevents FLUSHALL hang from idle
bolt connections. Removing it lets the server stay alive for
testBolt02_Regression which runs next.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(test): close bolt driver instead of stopping server in test99_cleanup

In ASAN mode, self.env.stop() kills the server but the port is not
released quickly enough. The next test class's __init__ starts a new
server that fails to bind to BOLT_PORT and dies with 'Redis server is
dead'. Fix by closing the bolt driver connection instead — this
prevents idle bolt connections from blocking FLUSHALL during RLTest
teardown while keeping the server alive for subsequent test classes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(test): add retry logic for BOLT_PORT binding in ASAN mode

In ASAN builds, the previous server takes extra time to release
BOLT_PORT after env.stop(). Add retry loop (5 attempts, 2s delay)
to _bolt_setup and testBolt04_WebSocket.__init__ so the next test
class can wait for port availability. Also close the bolt driver
before stopping the server in test99_cleanup to ensure clean
shutdown order.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(test): revert test99_cleanup to match master pattern

Remove _bolt_teardown() call before env.stop() in test99_cleanup.
Closing the driver before stopping the server triggers ASAN leak
reports: the server processes the disconnect, allocating temporary
state in the worker thread, then env.stop() kills the server before
cleanup completes. Master only calls env.stop() (no driver close),
which avoids the leak. The retry logic in _bolt_setup handles the
port-release race from the hard stop.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(test): merge bolt test classes into single testBolt class

Master uses a single testBolt class sharing one server process.
The 4-class split (testBolt01_Types, testBolt02_Regression,
testBolt03_Stress, testBolt04_WebSocket) caused each class to
start/stop its own server, producing ASAN leak reports at shutdown
and port-release races between classes.

Merging back to a single class eliminates:
- test99_cleanup / env.stop() between classes
- ASAN leak reports from forced server shutdown
- BOLT_PORT binding races in ASAN builds
- Retry logic workarounds in _bolt_setup

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(test): close bolt driver before WS tests to free server threads

The neo4j driver's connection pool holds idle connections that consume
all bolt thread pool threads. When the WS tests try to connect via raw
sockets, no server thread is available to process the HTTP Upgrade
request, causing a timeout.

Close the driver before the first WS test to release pooled connections
and free server threads.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(test): add delay after driver close for server thread cleanup

In ASAN mode, server bolt threads need time to process GOODBYE messages
and return to the pool after driver.close(). Without a delay, the
immediately following WS connection attempt finds no available threads.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(test): add retry logic to WS connect for ASAN thread release

The bolt server dedicates one thread per connection. After driver.close(),
server threads need time to process GOODBYE and return to the pool.
In ASAN mode this can take 10+ seconds.

Add retry logic (3 attempts × 10s timeout) to _ws_connect so the test
waits for a server thread to become available rather than failing
immediately on the first timeout.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(test): merge WS upgrade and bolt handshake into single test

Separate tests for WS upgrade and bolt-over-WS caused a race condition:
closing the first WS connection didn't give the server enough time to
clean up before the second connection attempted the bolt handshake.

Merging them into test30_ws_handshake_and_bolt uses a single connection
for both the upgrade and handshake, eliminating the race. Also adds
retry logic to _ws_connect for robustness in ASAN mode.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(test): limit bolt driver pool to 1 connection

The bolt server has a 4-thread pool. Each pooled driver connection holds
one thread. With the default pool size (100), idle connections can
exhaust all server threads, preventing WS test connections.

Setting max_connection_pool_size=1 ensures only 1 thread is used by the
driver, leaving 3 available for WS tests. This eliminates the need to
close the driver before WS tests and avoids timing-related races.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(test): remove unused hashlib import and use assertGreaterEqual

- Remove unused 'import hashlib' (CodeQL finding)
- Replace assertTrue(a >= b) with assertGreaterEqual(a, b) for better
  assertion messages

Addresses review comments from github-code-quality.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(test): correct Bolt version proposal byte order in WS test

The Bolt handshake version proposals use raw byte format
[patch, range, minor, major], not big-endian packed fields.
0x00070005 gave wire bytes [0x00,0x07,0x00,0x05] = minor=0,
causing bolt_read_supported_version to reject (minor < 1).
Fixed to 0x00000705 and 0x00000105.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(test): use websocket-client library for WS bolt tests

Replace hand-rolled WebSocket implementation (frame encoding/decoding,
HTTP upgrade handshake) with the websocket-client library for test30.

Keep test31 (raw TCP garbage) and test32 (custom Connection header
variants) as raw-socket tests since they test specific server-side
HTTP parsing edge cases that require exact control over the wire format.

commit.message:
Handle non-existent root directory in PhysicalFilesWatcher (#126411)

## Handle non-existent root directory in PhysicalFilesWatcher

Fixes #116713
Fixes #107700

### Problem

When `PhysicalFileProvider` is constructed with a root directory that
does not yet exist (e.g., a configuration file path whose parent
directory hasn't been created), `Watch()` fails because
`FileSystemWatcher` cannot watch a non-existent directory. This commonly
occurs with `AddJsonFile` when the config file's parent directory is
missing at startup.

`FileConfigurationSource` had its own solution: watching the closest
existing directory instead. The problem with this is that it commonly
watched too much of the system (possibly even all of it), causing
significant performance issues.

### Solution

`PhysicalFilesWatcher` now gracefully handles a missing root directory
by deferring `FileSystemWatcher` activation until the root appears. A
`PendingCreationWatcher` monitors the nearest existing ancestor
directory using a non-recursive `FileSystemWatcher` and cascades through
intermediate directory levels as they are created. Once the root
directory exists, the main recursive `FileSystemWatcher` is enabled and
any already-existing watched entries are reported.

Callers always receive normal FSW-backed change tokens — no
re-registration is needed when the root directory appears later.

`FileConfigurationSource` then uses `PhysicalFilesWatcher` on a
directory that may not exist, which is now handled well.

### Changes

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `PhysicalFilesWatcher.cs` | Added `PendingCreationWatcher` inner class
that watches for a non-existent directory to be created.
`TryEnableFileSystemWatcher` defers to `EnsureRootCreationWatcher` when
`_root` doesn't exist, with a callback to retry once it appears.
`ReportExistingWatchedEntries` fires tokens for entries created before
the FSW was active. The constructor normalizes `_root` to always have a
trailing separator and validates FSW path relationship.
`OnFileSystemEntryChange` now uses `DirectoryInfo` for directory paths
so exclusion filters work correctly, and guards against events outside
`_root`. `OnError` now also notifies wildcard tokens. |
| `PhysicalFileProvider.cs` | Constructor no longer throws
`DirectoryNotFoundException` for a missing root. Updated `Watch` doc
comments to include directories. Removed duplicate `_pathSeparators`
field in favor of `PathUtils.PathSeparators`. |
| `FileConfigurationSource.cs` | `ResolveFileProvider` creates the
`PhysicalFileProvider` with the file's immediate parent directory (even
if missing), relying on the watcher to handle the non-existent case. |
| `PollingFileChangeToken.cs` | `GetLastWriteTimeUtc` now falls back to
checking `DirectoryInfo` when `FileInfo.Exists` is false, so polling
correctly detects directory changes. `DirectoryInfo` is created lazily.
|
| `PathUtils.cs` | `PathSeparators` made `internal` for reuse across
files. |
| `PhysicalFilesWatcherTests.cs` | Tests for missing root (file path and
wildcard), root deleted and recreated, subdirectory
create/delete/recreate cycles, directory watch tokens, hidden directory
exclusion, FSW path above/below root, sibling directory prefix
isolation, and active polling variants. |
| `FileConfigurationProviderTest.cs` | Integration test verifying the
watch token fires when a file is created inside a previously-missing
directory. |
| `PhysicalFileProviderTests.cs` | Added test for constructing with
non-existent root. Fixed
`TokenFiredForGlobbingPatternsPointingToSubDirectory` path length issue
on .NET Framework. |

Fix iterator protocol dispatch for VmObject-wrapped native functions

The __forOfValues host function dispatches callable values by their
Value variant. make_native_fn creates Value::VmObject with __native_id__,
but the dispatch only checked for __host_fn__ in the VmObject branch.
This caused generators (whose .next/.return/.throw are native fn objects)
to fail with 'iterator.next is not callable' during array destructuring.

Added __native_id__ handling to all three VmObject dispatch points in
__forOfValues: Symbol.iterator call, .next() iteration, and .return()
cleanup. Also updated the duck-typing callable check for the undefined
Symbol.iterator fallback.

Fixes ~400+ Test262 failures across clusters 2-24 (array destructuring
with generators/custom iterables).

commit.message:
Simplify DinD DNS: use CoreDNS directly via nftables MASQUERADE

Now that nftables MASQUERADE works for 172.16.0.0/12, Docker containers
inside DinD can query K8s CoreDNS (10.43.0.10) directly. The MASQUERADE
rule rewrites the source IP (172.17.x.x → pod IP) so CoreDNS accepts the
queries. The socat UDP DNS proxy is no longer needed and has been removed.

The dind container now only needs nftables (no socat), simplifying startup.

README: add WIP notice, update architecture to reflect current state
(nftables MASQUERADE instead of socat DNS proxy, Ollama instead of Dynamo).

openshell gateway still fails 'Cluster DNS resolution failed' — under
investigation.
